# constant

```go
import "go/constant"
```

Package constant implements Values representing untyped Go constants and their corresponding operations.

A special Unknown value may be used when a value is unknown due to an error. Operations on unknown values produce unknown values unless specified otherwise.

<details><summary>Example (Complex Numbers)</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"go/constant"
	"go/token"
)

func main() {
	// Create the complex number 2.3 + 5i.
	ar := constant.MakeFloat64(2.3)
	ai := constant.MakeImag(constant.MakeInt64(5))
	a := constant.BinaryOp(ar, token.ADD, ai)

	// Compute (2.3 + 5i) * 11.
	b := constant.MakeUint64(11)
	c := constant.BinaryOp(a, token.MUL, b)

	// Convert c into a complex128.
	Ar, exact := constant.Float64Val(constant.Real(c))
	if !exact {
		fmt.Printf("Could not represent real part %s exactly as float64\n", constant.Real(c))
	}
	Ai, exact := constant.Float64Val(constant.Imag(c))
	if !exact {
		fmt.Printf("Could not represent imaginary part %s as exactly as float64\n", constant.Imag(c))
	}
	C := complex(Ar, Ai)

	fmt.Println("literal", 25.3+55i)
	fmt.Println("go/constant", c)
	fmt.Println("complex128", C)

}
```

#### Output

```go
Could not represent real part 25.3 exactly as float64
literal (25.3+55i)
go/constant (25.3 + 55i)
complex128 (25.299999999999997+55i)
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="BitLen"></a>

## func [BitLen](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L645>)

```go
func BitLen(x Value) int
```

BitLen returns the number of bits required to represent the absolute value x in binary representation; x must be an [Int](<#Unknown>) or an [Unknown](<#Unknown>). If x is [Unknown](<#Unknown>), the result is 0.

<a name="BoolVal"></a>

## func [BoolVal](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L480>)

```go
func BoolVal(x Value) bool
```

BoolVal returns the Go boolean value of x, which must be a [Bool](<#Unknown>) or an [Unknown](<#Unknown>). If x is [Unknown](<#Unknown>), the result is false.

<a name="Bytes"></a>

## func [Bytes](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L702>)

```go
func Bytes(x Value) []byte
```

Bytes returns the bytes for the absolute value of x in little\- endian binary representation; x must be an [Int](<#Unknown>).

<a name="Compare"></a>

## func [Compare](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L1340>)

```go
func Compare(x_ Value, op token.Token, y_ Value) bool
```

Compare returns the result of the comparison x op y. The comparison must be defined for the operands. If one of the operands is [Unknown](<#Unknown>), the result is false.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"go/constant"
	"go/token"
	"slices"
)

func main() {
	vs := []constant.Value{
		constant.MakeString("Z"),
		constant.MakeString("bacon"),
		constant.MakeString("go"),
		constant.MakeString("Frame"),
		constant.MakeString("defer"),
		constant.MakeFromLiteral(`"a"`, token.STRING, 0),
	}

	slices.SortFunc(vs, func(a, b constant.Value) int {
		if constant.Compare(a, token.LSS, b) {
			return -1
		}
		if constant.Compare(a, token.GTR, b) {
			return +1
		}
		return 0
	})

	for _, v := range vs {
		fmt.Println(constant.StringVal(v))
	}

}
```

#### Output

```go
Frame
Z
a
bacon
defer
go
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Float32Val"></a>

## func [Float32Val](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L537>)

```go
func Float32Val(x Value) (float32, bool)
```

Float32Val is like [Float64Val](<#Float64Val>) but for float32 instead of float64.

<a name="Float64Val"></a>

## func [Float64Val](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L562>)

```go
func Float64Val(x Value) (float64, bool)
```

Float64Val returns the nearest Go float64 value of x and whether the result is exact; x must be numeric or an [Unknown](<#Unknown>), but not [Complex](<#Unknown>). For values too small \(too close to 0\) to represent as float64, [Float64Val](<#Float64Val>) silently underflows to 0. The result sign always matches the sign of x, even for 0. If x is [Unknown](<#Unknown>), the result is \(0, false\).

<a name="Int64Val"></a>

## func [Int64Val](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L507>)

```go
func Int64Val(x Value) (int64, bool)
```

Int64Val returns the Go int64 value of x and whether the result is exact; x must be an [Int](<#Unknown>) or an [Unknown](<#Unknown>). If the result is not exact, its value is undefined. If x is [Unknown](<#Unknown>), the result is \(0, false\).

<a name="Sign"></a>

## func [Sign](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L665>)

```go
func Sign(x Value) int
```

Sign returns \-1, 0, or 1 depending on whether x \< 0, x == 0, or x \> 0; x must be numeric or [Unknown](<#Unknown>). For complex values x, the sign is 0 if x == 0, otherwise it is \!= 0. If x is [Unknown](<#Unknown>), the result is 1.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"go/constant"
	"go/token"
)

func main() {
	zero := constant.MakeInt64(0)
	one := constant.MakeInt64(1)
	negOne := constant.MakeInt64(-1)

	mkComplex := func(a, b constant.Value) constant.Value {
		b = constant.MakeImag(b)
		return constant.BinaryOp(a, token.ADD, b)
	}

	vs := []constant.Value{
		negOne,
		mkComplex(zero, negOne),
		mkComplex(one, negOne),
		mkComplex(negOne, one),
		mkComplex(negOne, negOne),
		zero,
		mkComplex(zero, zero),
		one,
		mkComplex(zero, one),
		mkComplex(one, one),
	}

	for _, v := range vs {
		fmt.Printf("% d %s\n", constant.Sign(v), v)
	}

}
```

#### Output

```go
-1 -1
-1 (0 + -1i)
-1 (1 + -1i)
-1 (-1 + 1i)
-1 (-1 + -1i)
 0 0
 0 (0 + 0i)
 1 1
 1 (0 + 1i)
 1 (1 + 1i)
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="StringVal"></a>

## func [StringVal](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L493>)

```go
func StringVal(x Value) string
```

StringVal returns the Go string value of x, which must be a [String](<#Unknown>) or an [Unknown](<#Unknown>). If x is [Unknown](<#Unknown>), the result is "".

<a name="Uint64Val"></a>

## func [Uint64Val](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L523>)

```go
func Uint64Val(x Value) (uint64, bool)
```

Uint64Val returns the Go uint64 value of x and whether the result is exact; x must be an [Int](<#Unknown>) or an [Unknown](<#Unknown>). If the result is not exact, its value is undefined. If x is [Unknown](<#Unknown>), the result is \(0, false\).

<a name="Val"></a>

## func [Val](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L593>)

```go
func Val(x Value) any
```

Val returns the underlying value for a given constant. Since it returns an interface, it is up to the caller to type assert the result to the expected type. The possible dynamic return types are:

```go
x Kind             type of result
-----------------------------------------
Bool               bool
String             string
Int                int64 or *big.Int
Float              *big.Float or *big.Rat
everything else    nil
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"go/constant"
	"math"
)

func main() {
	maxint := constant.MakeInt64(math.MaxInt64)
	fmt.Printf("%v\n", constant.Val(maxint))

	e := constant.MakeFloat64(math.E)
	fmt.Printf("%v\n", constant.Val(e))

	b := constant.MakeBool(true)
	fmt.Printf("%v\n", constant.Val(b))

	b = constant.Make(false)
	fmt.Printf("%v\n", constant.Val(b))

}
```

#### Output

```go
9223372036854775807
6121026514868073/2251799813685248
true
false
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Kind"></a>

## type [Kind](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L29>)

Kind specifies the kind of value represented by a [Value](<#Value>).

```go
type Kind int
```

<a name="Unknown"></a>

```go
const (
    // unknown values
    Unknown Kind = iota

    // non-numeric values
    Bool
    String

    // numeric values
    Int
    Float
    Complex
)
```

<a name="Kind.String"></a>

### func \(Kind\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/kind_string.go#L23>)

```go
func (i Kind) String() string
```

<a name="Value"></a>

## type [Value](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L46-L62>)

A Value represents the value of a Go constant.

```go
type Value interface {
    // Kind returns the value kind.
    Kind() Kind

    // String returns a short, quoted (human-readable) form of the value.
    // For numeric values, the result may be an approximation;
    // for String values the result may be a shortened string.
    // Use ExactString for a string representing a value exactly.
    String() string

    // ExactString returns an exact, quoted (human-readable) form of the value.
    // If the Value is of Kind String, use StringVal to obtain the unquoted string.
    ExactString() string
    // contains filtered or unexported methods
}
```

<a name="BinaryOp"></a>

### func [BinaryOp](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L1106>)

```go
func BinaryOp(x_ Value, op token.Token, y_ Value) Value
```

BinaryOp returns the result of the binary expression x op y. The operation must be defined for the operands. If one of the operands is [Unknown](<#Unknown>), the result is [Unknown](<#Unknown>). BinaryOp doesn't handle comparisons or shifts; use [Compare](<#Compare>) or [Shift](<#Shift>) instead.

To force integer division of [Int](<#Unknown>) operands, use op == \[token.QUO\_ASSIGN\] instead of \[token.QUO\]; the result is guaranteed to be [Int](<#Unknown>) in this case. Division by zero leads to a run\-time panic.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"go/constant"
	"go/token"
)

func main() {
	// 11 / 0.5
	a := constant.MakeUint64(11)
	b := constant.MakeFloat64(0.5)
	c := constant.BinaryOp(a, token.QUO, b)
	fmt.Println(c)

}
```

#### Output

```go
22
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Denom"></a>

### func [Denom](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L788>)

```go
func Denom(x Value) Value
```

Denom returns the denominator of x; x must be [Int](<#Unknown>), [Float](<#Unknown>), or [Unknown](<#Unknown>). If x is [Unknown](<#Unknown>), or if it is too large or small to represent as a fraction, the result is [Unknown](<#Unknown>). Otherwise the result is an [Int](<#Unknown>) \>= 1.

<a name="Imag"></a>

### func [Imag](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L836>)

```go
func Imag(x Value) Value
```

Imag returns the imaginary part of x, which must be a numeric or unknown value. If x is [Unknown](<#Unknown>), the result is [Unknown](<#Unknown>).

<a name="Make"></a>

### func [Make](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L623>)

```go
func Make(x any) Value
```

Make returns the [Value](<#Value>) for x.

```go
type of x        result Kind
----------------------------
bool             Bool
string           String
int64            Int
*big.Int         Int
*big.Float       Float
*big.Rat         Float
anything else    Unknown
```

<a name="MakeBool"></a>

### func [MakeBool](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L386>)

```go
func MakeBool(b bool) Value
```

MakeBool returns the [Bool](<#Unknown>) value for b.

<a name="MakeFloat64"></a>

### func [MakeFloat64](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L412>)

```go
func MakeFloat64(x float64) Value
```

MakeFloat64 returns the [Float](<#Unknown>) value for x. If x is \-0.0, the result is 0.0. If x is not finite, the result is an [Unknown](<#Unknown>).

<a name="MakeFromBytes"></a>

### func [MakeFromBytes](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L734>)

```go
func MakeFromBytes(bytes []byte) Value
```

MakeFromBytes returns the [Int](<#Unknown>) value given the bytes of its little\-endian binary representation. An empty byte slice argument represents 0.

<a name="MakeFromLiteral"></a>

### func [MakeFromLiteral](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L427>)

```go
func MakeFromLiteral(lit string, tok token.Token, zero uint) Value
```

MakeFromLiteral returns the corresponding integer, floating\-point, imaginary, character, or string value for a Go literal string. The tok value must be one of \[token.INT\], \[token.FLOAT\], \[token.IMAG\], \[token.CHAR\], or \[token.STRING\]. The final argument must be zero. If the literal string syntax is invalid, the result is an [Unknown](<#Unknown>).

<a name="MakeImag"></a>

### func [MakeImag](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L810>)

```go
func MakeImag(x Value) Value
```

MakeImag returns the [Complex](<#Unknown>) value x\*i; x must be [Int](<#Unknown>), [Float](<#Unknown>), or [Unknown](<#Unknown>). If x is [Unknown](<#Unknown>), the result is [Unknown](<#Unknown>).

### func [MakeInt64](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L399>)

```go
func MakeInt64(x int64) Value
```

MakeInt64 returns the [Int](<#Unknown>) value for x.

<a name="MakeUnknown"></a>

### func [MakeUnknown](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L383>)

<a name="Num"></a>

### func [Num](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L766>)

```go
func Num(x Value) Value
```

Num returns the numerator of x; x must be [Int](<#Unknown>), [Float](<#Unknown>), or [Unknown](<#Unknown>). If x is [Unknown](<#Unknown>), or if it is too large or small to represent as a fraction, the result is [Unknown](<#Unknown>). Otherwise the result is an [Int](<#Unknown>) with the same sign as x.

<a name="Real"></a>

### func [Real](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L823>)

```go
func Real(x Value) Value
```

Real returns the real part of x, which must be a numeric or unknown value. If x is [Unknown](<#Unknown>), the result is [Unknown](<#Unknown>).

<a name="Shift"></a>

### func [Shift](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L1285>)

```go
func Shift(x Value, op token.Token, s uint) Value
```

Shift returns the result of the shift expression x op s with op == \[token.SHL\] or \[token.SHR\] \(\<\< or \>\>\). x must be an [Int](<#Unknown>) or an [Unknown](<#Unknown>). If x is [Unknown](<#Unknown>), the result is x.

<a name="ToComplex"></a>

### func [ToComplex](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L929>)

```go
func ToComplex(x Value) Value
```

ToComplex converts x to a [Complex](<#Unknown>) value if x is representable as a [Complex](<#Unknown>). Otherwise it returns an [Unknown](<#Unknown>).

<a name="ToFloat"></a>

### func [ToFloat](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L908>)

```go
func ToFloat(x Value) Value
```

ToFloat converts x to a [Float](<#Unknown>) value if x is representable as a [Float](<#Unknown>). Otherwise it returns an [Unknown](<#Unknown>).

<a name="ToInt"></a>

### func [ToInt](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L854>)

```go
func ToInt(x Value) Value
```

ToInt converts x to an [Int](<#Unknown>) value if x is representable as an [Int](<#Unknown>). Otherwise it returns an [Unknown](<#Unknown>).

<a name="UnaryOp"></a>

### func [UnaryOp](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/constant/value.go#L958>)

```go
func UnaryOp(op token.Token, y Value, prec uint) Value
```

UnaryOp returns the result of the unary expression op y. The operation must be defined for the operand. If prec \> 0 it specifies the ^ \(xor\) result size in bits. If y is [Unknown](<#Unknown>), the result is [Unknown](<#Unknown>).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"go/constant"
	"go/token"
)

func main() {
	vs := []constant.Value{
		constant.MakeBool(true),
		constant.MakeFloat64(2.7),
		constant.MakeUint64(42),
	}

	for i, v := range vs {
		switch v.Kind() {
		case constant.Bool:
			vs[i] = constant.UnaryOp(token.NOT, v, 0)

		case constant.Float:
			vs[i] = constant.UnaryOp(token.SUB, v, 0)

		case constant.Int:
			// Use 16-bit precision.
			// This would be equivalent to ^uint16(v).
			vs[i] = constant.UnaryOp(token.XOR, v, 16)
		}
	}

	for _, v := range vs {
		fmt.Println(v)
	}

}
```

#### Output

```go
false
-2.7
65493
```

</p>
</details>
